title: Command and Scripting Interpreter
description: Hunt for suspicious shell, script, and interpreter execution that suggests hands-on-keyboard activity or staged execution.
hypothesis_templates: An adversary used a command or scripting interpreter to execute suspicious actions on a monitored host.
Encoded or parent-process-anomalous shell execution indicates post-compromise activity.

parameters.name: tenant
parameters.description: Tenant, business unit, or environment selector.

parameters.name: lookback_hours
parameters.description: Hours of endpoint and SIEM telemetry to review.

parameters.name: focus_host
parameters.description: Optional hostname or endpoint identifier to prioritize.

telemetry_requirements.description: Process creation, command-line, parent-child lineage, and host context.
SIEM-level command execution events and correlated detections.

blind_spots: Hosts without endpoint sensors or with truncated command-line logging can hide interpreter abuse.
Living-off-the-land activity that never emits distinct process telemetry may blend into normal automation.

execution_targets.name: CrowdStrike suspicious interpreter process hunt
execution_targets.description: Search endpoint process telemetry for suspicious shells, interpreters, and encoded command patterns.
execution_targets.query_template: event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2 aid:* CommandLine=*{{focus_host}}* OR CommandLine=*powershell* OR CommandLine=*cmd.exe*

execution_targets.name: Splunk interpreter execution pivot
execution_targets.description: Correlate suspicious interpreter launches and alert context in SIEM telemetry.
execution_targets.query_template: index=endpoint tenant={{tenant}} (process_name=powershell.exe OR process_name=cmd.exe OR process_name=wscript.exe) earliest=-{{lookback_hours}}h

execution_targets.name: Elastic ES|QL interpreter sweep
execution_targets.description: Use ES|QL to find anomalous interpreter execution with parent or command-line pivots.
execution_targets.query_template: FROM logs-endpoint-* | WHERE tenant == "{{tenant}}" AND process.name IN ("powershell.exe", "cmd.exe", "wscript.exe")

notes: Use this pack when initial telemetry suggests suspicious scripting or shell execution.
